subject: Computer Networking
title: Bandwidth vs. Latency
description: A short lesson to help you apply Bandwidth vs. Latency.

hook.text: Your download is fast but your video call lags. Speed and responsiveness aren't the same thing.

buildup.text: Bandwidth is how much data fits through a connection per second. Think of it as a pipe's width.

discovery.text: Latency is how long a single piece of data takes to travel from A to B. That's the pipe's length.

twist.text: Streaming needs bandwidth. Gaming needs low latency. Different apps care about different things.

climax.text: A satellite link has huge bandwidth but high latency. A fiber link has both — that's why fiber wins.

punchline.text: Bandwidth fills the bucket. Latency decides when.

quiz.question: What does latency measure?
options: The time it takes data to travel from source to destination
The maximum amount of data a connection can carry
The number of devices on a network
